item.title: Theme Documentation - Basics
item.description:
Gokarna is an opinionated theme with a focus on minimalism and simplicity.

Installation

Follow these steps to initialize your new website.

If you are new to Hugo, we suggest following this great documentation for beginners.

a. Create Your Project

Hugo provides a new subcommand to create a new website:

hugo new site my-website
cd my-website

b. Install the Theme

The theme’s repository is: https://github.com/526avijitgupta/gokarna.

item.title: Theme Documentation - Advanced
item.description:
Gokarna is an opinionated theme with a focus on minimalism and simplicity.

Content Types

This theme supports two types of content types: post and page. To specify them, you need to add them in your markdown metadata.

Post

This is the default blog post type which will be shown in your “Posts” section and who’s tags will be indexed. Basically, a normal blog post.

---
title: "Hello, world!"
date: 2021-01-01
description: "A blog post"
image: "/path/to/image.png"
type: "post"
tags: ["blog"]
---

# Hello World!
This is my blog.

Page

We introduced this type to distinguish between blog posts and normal markdown pages. The reason to create this was to give the user complete freedom in creating their website. You can use this to create a portfolio of your projects or showcase your designs. The possibilites are endless and the choice is yours.

item.title: Markdown Syntax Guide
item.description: This article offers a sample of basic Markdown syntax that can be used in Hugo content files, also it shows whether basic HTML elements are decorated with CSS in a Hugo theme.

item.title: Emoji Support
item.description:
Emoji can be enabled in a Hugo project in a number of ways.

The emojify function can be called directly in templates or Inline Shortcodes.

To enable emoji globally, set enableEmoji to true in your site configuration and then you can type emoji shorthand codes directly in content files.

These begin and end with a colon and include the code of an emoji:

Gone camping! {?:}tent: Be back soon.

That is so funny! {?:}joy:

The rendered output looks like this:

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hi!
I am a recent PhD graduate from ISAE-SUPAERO, Toulouse, France, where I specialized at the intersection of machine learning and computational fluid dynamics (CFD). My passion lies in bridging these two disciplines to develop innovative solutions for complex aerodynamic challenges.

Prior to my PhD, I earned a Master’s degree in Fluid Dynamics and Energetics from Toulouse INP - ENSEEIHT (France) in 2020, where I built a strong foundation in CFD, turbulence modeling, and aerodynamics.

item.title: Curriculum Vitae
item.description:
My CV

You can download my CV using the link below:

Download my CV

item.title: Publications
item.description:
title: Vortex Gust Mitigation Using Deep Reinforcement Learning

author: Brice Martin

date: 2024-11

publication_url: “To come”

Abstract: How to leverage deep reinforcement learning to solve vortex gust mitigation problems? In this dissertation, we approach the gust mitigation problem as an optimal control problem, modeled as a Markov decision process. Deep reinforcement learning is particularly well suited to solving these problems: first, it allows us to derive control functions regardless of the complexity of the aerodynamic model; second, the control function adapts to the fluid’s response to the control. However, for optimal performance, the controller needs to know in advance the locations and intensities of the unmeasurable gust vortices. Without this information, its efficiency is decreased. To address this issue, we employ a vortex estimation technique based on Kalman filters, which estimates the gust vortices using data from onboard sensors. Additionally, the learned control functions provide valuable insights into the mechanisms of gust mitigation. We reveal them using force partitioning techniques. Overall, this dissertation presents data-driven solutions for the dual tasks of vortex gust detection and mitigation, with a particular focus on understanding the physical mechanisms involved in these mitigation strategies.
